title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · Command Brief: Name Resolution Path
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux name resolution path increment in a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
Interpret interface addresses, routes, neighbors, and listening or established sockets as different network evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Name Resolution Path
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Name Resolution Path contract for the supplied data.
